clip: data/tennis/5.mp4 (two segments: frames 0-135 original camera position, frames 400-939 shifted ~70-100px after a real one-way pan -- NOT locked-off, and NOT the same 2-segment shape as 2.mp4/4.mp4)

TL.matches: far-left doubles corner, numeric trace, Segment A -- re-measured after a mean-of-cluster bug in the first pass put this at (611,273.5), off by ~85px; caught via cross-validation against the other 7 points, see reference_video5_calibration.py docstring

BL.matches: near-left doubles corner, numeric trace, Segment A -- matches frame 100 within 1px

BR.matches: near-right doubles corner, numeric trace, Segment B

TL.matches: far-left doubles corner, numeric trace, Segment B -- matches frames 450/600 within 1-2px

confirmed_note: Verified 2026-07-19. This clip has a real, one-way camera pan (NOT locked-off like 1.mp4/3.mp4, and NOT the same there-and-back 2-segment shape as 2.mp4, nor 4.mp4's multi-segment there-and-back-and-overshoot pattern) -- confirmed via 3 independent pixel-tracked points and a red/green frame-overlay visual check (frame 0 vs frame 320 vs frame 900). Two long, stable, position-matched-within-each-other windows exist: frames 0-135 (Segment A, BL reads 137.0/137.0/137.0 at frames 100/115/130) and frames 400-939 (Segment B, BL reads 212.0/212.0/212.0 at frames 450/600/900) -- these do NOT match each other (Segment A BL ~137 vs Segment B BL ~212, a real ~75px shift), confirming two independent calibrations are required, unlike 4.mp4 where the two stable windows turned out to be the SAME position. A real measurement bug was found and fixed during this verification: Segment A's first-pass TL read as (611.0, 273.5) via a mean-of-bright-cluster method that is invalid when the scan row also intersects the singles sideline crossing the same baseline; cross-validating TL against a homography built from the other 7 points (predicted position (537, 282), 74px from the first-pass reading) exposed this, and the corrected reading (526.0, 275.0) dropped Segment A's held-out error from 10.22/17.98px to 0.75/0.30px. A second, project-wide bug was found and fixed in the tooling itself: calibration_verification.py's render_verification_frames previously used cv2.CAP_PROP_POS_FRAMES seeking, which was found to silently return the WRONG frame's content past a keyframe boundary on every reference clip tested (1.mp4-5.mp4 all reproduce it -- e.g. video4.json's own frame 300 does not match a sequential read at all, maxdiff=255 out of 255). Fixed by switching to pure sequential decoding (_frames_at) for this module's frame extraction; all 6 rendered check images for this manifest (calib_check_frame{100,115,130,450,600,900}.jpg in cv_pipeline/scratch_output/tennis5/calib_verify/manifest_frames/) were confirmed via that fixed path and show all 4 corners landing tightly on the real court lines. Flagging the historical exposure as a backlog item, not re-auditing 1-4.mp4 retroactively right now, since none of those clips are failing their held-out thresholds.
